# Copilot Instructions for Sena-Meta-Team

This file provides instructions for GitHub Copilot when working in this repository.

## General Guidelines

- Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code
- Follow established coding conventions and best practices for the language being used
- Include meaningful comments where necessary to explain complex logic
- Ensure code is properly formatted and follows consistent style

## Code Quality

- Write self-documenting code with descriptive variable and function names
- Keep functions focused and single-purpose
- Handle errors appropriately and provide meaningful error messages
- Avoid code duplication - refactor common logic into reusable functions

## Testing

- Write tests for new functionality when test infrastructure exists
- Ensure existing tests pass before submitting changes
- Include edge cases in test coverage

## Documentation

- Update documentation when making changes that affect usage
- Include inline comments for complex algorithms or business logic
- Keep README and other documentation up to date

## Pull Requests

- Make small, focused changes that are easy to review
- Provide clear descriptions of what changes were made and why
- Reference related issues when applicable
